project.name: discreet-dynasties
project.description: TFOS satellite for family legacy and stewardship: pathways, vows, fate audits, armory, companion AI, hall community, social posting, content pipeline.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template listing all required environment variables including database URL, auth secrets, Stripe pricing keys, Anthropic/OpenAI API keys, Resend email key, and Vercel Blob token.

nodes.name: governance-checks.yml
nodes.summary: CI workflow that blocks pushes and PRs containing local cron entries in vercel.json, enforcing the rule that all crons must be managed through the orchestrator.

nodes.name: notify-deploy.yml
nodes.summary: CI workflow triggered on push that sends a deployment notification, likely to the orchestrator or team channel.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project-level governance document referencing the TFOS Master Forme, declaring the site's purpose (family legacy and stewardship), tech stack, and the mandatory handoff sanity check protocol.

nodes.name: PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive site architecture plan covering the FATE model, Dynasty Companion AI advisors, Armory generators, pathways, assessments, subscription tiers, DB schema, content seeding, and a 6-phase technical roadmap.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README documenting the site's mission within TFOS, core features (FATE audit, Companion, Armory, pathways), tech stack, directory structure, data flows, subscription tiers, setup instructions, and deployment process.

nodes.name: AccountBillingButton.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component that opens a Stripe billing portal session, allowing subscribers to manage their payment method and subscription.

nodes.name: AccountUsernameForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Client form component that lets authenticated users set or update their username with validation feedback and API submission.

nodes.name: handleSave
nodes.summary: Validates username input, submits it to the account username API, and handles success/error states with user feedback.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-rendered account dashboard page that displays the user's profile, subscription tier, role, and embeds the username form and billing button components.

nodes.name: EmailBlastForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin client component providing a form to compose and send segmented email blasts to users, with subject, body, and audience segment selection.

nodes.name: handleSend
nodes.summary: Submits the email blast form data to the admin blast API endpoint with confirmation dialog and error handling.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin page that renders the email blast form for composing and sending targeted email campaigns to site users.

nodes.name: GiftCreator.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin client component for creating gift codes with selectable durations (1 week to walking pass), optional recipient email, and copy-to-clipboard functionality.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: Submits gift code creation request to the admin gift API with type, email, and note parameters, then displays the generated code.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin page that lists all existing gift codes with their status, redemption info, and expiration dates, and embeds the GiftCreator component for generating new codes.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin layout component that gates access to admin-only users via session check and renders the admin navigation shell.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin dashboard landing page displaying key site metrics including user counts, subscription breakdowns, and recent activity summaries.

nodes.name: UserRoleSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin client component providing a dropdown to change a user's role, submitting the update via PATCH to the admin users API.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin user management page listing all registered users with their roles, subscription tiers, and inline role editing via UserRoleSelect.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route handling POST requests to update the authenticated user's username with trimming and validation in the database.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin API route that sends segmented email blasts via Resend to filtered user segments, with admin-only access control and Prisma-based user querying.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates admin session, filters users by segment criteria, and dispatches individual emails via Resend with error tracking per recipient.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin API route with POST for creating gift codes (with random code generation, optional recipient email, and type-based expiration) and GET for listing all gift codes.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Creates a new gift code with generated unique code, stores it in the database with expiration logic based on gift type, and optionally sends an email to the recipient.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin API route handling PATCH requests to update a specific user's role by ID, with admin-only access control.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route powering the Armory AI generators (FATE roadmap, stability plan, food storage calculator, dead preps audit, and more) by dispatching user inputs to Anthropic Claude with domain-specific prompts and tier-gated access.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a prompt registry pattern mapping generator slugs to template functions, enabling clean extension of new generator types.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the user, validates their subscription tier against the requested generator, assembles the prompt from the registry, and streams the Anthropic response back to the client.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth.js catch-all route that re-exports the auth handler, delegating all authentication logic to the shared auth configuration.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route that creates a Stripe billing portal session for the authenticated user, redirecting them to manage their subscription and payment method.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route that creates a Stripe Checkout session for subscription purchases, mapping the requested price ID to a checkout flow with success/cancel URLs.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for the Dynasty Companion AI chat, supporting multiple advisor personas and council mode with RAG-powered book search, tier-gated advisor access, and Anthropic streaming responses.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the user, performs RAG search against the book content, builds the advisor-specific system prompt, and streams the Anthropic response for the Dynasty Companion chat.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Cron-triggered API route that generates and publishes new content articles via the content pipeline, with kill switch, cron auth verification, and owner notification.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Verifies cron authentication, checks the kill switch, then delegates to the content pipeline to generate and publish a new article, logging the result to CronLog.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Cron-triggered API route implementing the evergreen social posting engine -- extracts book passages via cursor, generates platform-specific social copy with AI, deduplicates via passage hashing, and dispatches to all configured social platforms.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a cursor-based chunking system to systematically walk through book content, ensuring every passage gets social coverage without repetition.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full evergreen social posting cycle: auth check, kill switch, cursor retrieval, passage extraction, AI copy generation, dedup check, multi-platform dispatch, cursor advancement, and comprehensive cron logging.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Cron handler that publishes scheduled dispatches by flipping their published flag when publishedAt time arrives, with full cron logging.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Authenticated GET handler that finds dispatches due for publication, marks them published, and logs execution to cronLog.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Multi-phase social media dispatch cron that generates AI copy for new dispatches, handles scheduled posts, detects story arcs, and fetches platform engagement metrics across Facebook, Instagram, and X.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses multi-model AI pipeline: Claude for copy generation, optional Grok refinement for X/Twitter posts. Supports kill switch via SOCIAL_CRON_ENABLED env var.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Orchestrates four social posting phases: scheduled post dispatch, new dispatch social copy generation with AI, story-arc detection, and retrospective metrics collection.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Saves a user's FATE audit results (Food, Assurance, Tools, Energy) to the database with stability/continuity/integrity scores per domain.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Gift code redemption endpoint that validates a code, assigns the user a membership tier with optional expiration, and marks the code as redeemed in a transaction.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Creates comments on Hall posts with paid-tier auth gating and content validation including length limits and threading support.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Toggle-style like endpoint for Hall posts using unique compound key (postId + userId) to prevent duplicates.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Creates new Hall posts with paid-tier auth gating, content validation, category tagging, and journal-mode support.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Health check endpoint that verifies database connectivity and Stripe availability, returning status with version and build SHA for orchestrator monitoring.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Checks database and Stripe connectivity, aggregates results into a healthy/degraded status response with site metadata.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Advances a user's Long Table progress by one level (max 6), using upsert to handle both new and returning users.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Marks a pathway week as complete and advances user to the next week, supporting both JSON and form POST. Redirects back to the pathway page on completion.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates pathway slug and week number, then creates or updates pathway progress record, marking the pathway complete when the final week is finished.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Records or renews a Dynast-tier user's Vow commitment with optional display name, state, and public visibility toggle.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Stripe webhook handler that upgrades users on checkout.session.completed and downgrades on subscription cancellation, following the 200-only external webhook policy.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Verifies Stripe webhook signature, processes checkout completion (tier upgrade) and subscription deletion (tier reset), always returning 200 to prevent webhook suspension.

nodes.name: ArmoryGenerator.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side AI generator component that collects user inputs via dynamic form fields, streams the AI response, and displays the result with copy and regenerate actions.

nodes.name: ArmoryGenerator
nodes.summary: React component rendering a dynamic form based on generator field definitions, submitting to the armory API, and streaming the AI-generated response with live preview.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server page for individual Armory generators, defining 10 generator configurations (FATE roadmap, stability plan, food storage, etc.) with tier-gated access and JSON-LD structured data.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a large static GENERATORS configuration object defining all 10 dynasty generator tools with their field schemas and tier requirements.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Armory index page displaying all 10 AI generator tools in a grid, with tier-based locking (Builder vs Steward) and a paywall for free users.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive FATE Audit assessment page where users answer yes/no questions across four domains (Food, Assurance, Tools, Energy) at three levels, then view domain scores with next-step recommendations.

nodes.name: FateAuditPage
nodes.summary: Client component rendering a multi-domain questionnaire with real-time answer tracking, score computation on submit, and results view showing weakest domain with actionable next steps.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Sign-in page offering magic link authentication via Resend email and GitHub OAuth as an alternative, styled in the Dynasty Forge design language.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Static 'check your email' confirmation page shown after magic link sign-in request.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Individual chapter reader page that fetches book content from the database, renders Markdown with GFM support, and provides prev/next navigation and a Companion CTA.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Book table of contents page listing all 28 chapters organized by section, with tier-based locking and a membership upsell for free users.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynasty Companion AI chat interface supporting three conversation modes: archetype-based, single historical advisor, and council mode with two advisors debating. Includes URL-based advisor preselection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Suspense boundary for useSearchParams compatibility in Next.js App Router. Supports 13 historical/biblical advisor personas.

nodes.name: CompanionInner
nodes.summary: Core companion chat component managing conversation state, advisor/archetype/council mode selection, message send/receive lifecycle, and a settings panel for switching between 13 historical advisors.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: User dashboard ('My Forge') showing FATE audit scores, active pathway progress with week-by-week visualization, quick action links, and upgrade CTA for free users.

nodes.name: ForgePage
nodes.summary: Server component that fetches user's latest FATE audit and active pathway progress, rendering a personalized dashboard with scores, pathway status bars, quick actions, and tier-gated features.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Council of Advisors gallery page displaying 13 historical and biblical advisor personas organized by group, with links to open individual or council-mode companion sessions.

nodes.name: GiftRedemptionClient.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side gift claim button that calls the gift redemption API and redirects to the forge dashboard on success.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Gift code landing page that validates the code, shows appropriate states (not found, already claimed, or ready to claim), and renders the redemption client for authenticated users.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Dynasty Forge design system stylesheet defining a walnut-and-amber color palette with light/dark themes, prose typography for book reading, page transitions, scrollbar styling, and accessibility features.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties for theming with shadcn/ui variable mappings. Includes prefers-reduced-motion and prefers-contrast media queries for accessibility.

nodes.name: HallCommentForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Client comment form component for Hall posts with submit handling, error display, and router refresh on success to show the new comment.

nodes.name: HallLikeButton.tsx
nodes.summary: Optimistic like toggle button for Hall posts that immediately updates the UI count and reverts on API failure.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-rendered Hall post detail page displaying a single community post with threaded comments, likes, and reply form. Requires paid membership to access.

nodes.name: HallPostPage
nodes.summary: Async server component that loads a Hall post with nested comments and likes from Prisma, enforces paid-tier access, and renders the full post detail view.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side form for creating new Hall community posts or private journal entries, with category selection, character limit, and journal toggle.

nodes.name: HallCreateInner
nodes.summary: Client component rendering the post creation form with category selection, content textarea with 2000-char limit, and private journal toggle. Submits to /api/hall/posts.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: The Hall listing page showing recent community posts for paid members, with a paywall gate redirecting free users to the join page.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Subscription redirect page that directs users to Stoic Preparedness for unified TFOS subscription management, with FAQ section explaining cross-site access.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Private journal page displaying the authenticated user's personal journal entries created via The Hall's journal mode, with date formatting and category labels.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root layout configuring Crimson Pro, Outfit, and IBM Plex Mono fonts, structured data (JSON-LD), session/theme providers, navigation, and footer for the entire site.

nodes.name: LongTableProgressForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Small client component providing a button to advance the user's Long Table level by calling /api/long-table/advance, with completion state display.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: The Long Table framework page presenting six concentric levels of household-to-civic resilience, with visual progress tracking and level advancement for paid members.

nodes.name: LongTablePage
nodes.summary: Async server component rendering the Long Table six-level progression framework with user progress from Prisma, visual progress bar, and level cards with action links.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Marketing homepage for Discreet Dynasties with hero section, four entry paths (Book, FATE Audit, Pathways, Armory), FATE model overview, Dynasty Companion teaser, and closing CTA.

nodes.name: HomePage
nodes.summary: Top-level homepage component rendering the full marketing page with hero, mission statement, four feature cards, FATE model explainer, Companion teaser, and closing quote.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynamic pathway detail page rendering a guided multi-week program with progress tracking, week cards with actions and reflections, and JSON-LD structured data.

nodes.name: PathwayPage
nodes.summary: Async server component loading pathway data and user progress from Prisma, enforcing tier-based access, and rendering week-by-week program cards with completion controls.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Pathways listing page showing all six guided programs with tier-gated access indicators, routing paid users to individual pathways and free users to the join page.

nodes.name: robots.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js robots.txt configuration allowing public crawling while disallowing admin, API, forge, account, and foundry routes.

nodes.name: sitemap.ts
nodes.summary: Dynamic sitemap generator producing URLs for static pages, all book chapters, and all pathways with appropriate change frequencies and priorities.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: The Vow ceremony page displaying the dynasty-building commitment text, existing vow status for Dynast members, take-the-vow CTA, and public registry of members who have taken it.

nodes.name: TheVowPage
nodes.summary: Server component rendering The Vow commitment page with tier-gated access, existing vow lookup, public registry display, and success banner after taking the vow.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side form for formally taking The Vow commitment, with optional display name, state/region, public listing toggle, and confirmation checkbox before submission.

nodes.name: TakeTheVowPage
nodes.summary: Client component managing the vow-taking ceremony form with display name, region, public listing toggle, confirmation checkbox, and POST to /api/vow/take.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth v5 configuration using Prisma adapter with Resend (magic link) and GitHub providers, session callback enriching user data with role, paid status, and gift access expiry handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses NextAuth v5 pattern exporting { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } for App Router integration.

nodes.name: book.md
nodes.summary: Complete text of 'Discreet Dynasties' by F. Tronboll III -- a 4961-line book covering the FATE model, Long Table framework, Gray Man principle, and dynasty-building philosophy used as the doctrinal source for the AI companion and content pipeline.

nodes.name: Footer.tsx
nodes.summary: Site-wide footer component with four-column layout linking to Forge features, community pages, and external Tronboll family sites.

nodes.name: Navigation.tsx
nodes.summary: Responsive navigation bar with desktop/mobile layouts, session-aware auth controls (sign in/out, user dropdown), theme toggle, and links to all major site sections.

nodes.name: Navigation
nodes.summary: Client component rendering a sticky header with responsive desktop/mobile navigation, session-based user menu dropdown, and theme toggle integration.

nodes.name: Providers.tsx
nodes.summary: Client provider wrapper combining NextAuth SessionProvider and next-themes ThemeProvider for the entire application tree.

nodes.name: ThemeToggle.tsx
nodes.summary: Light/dark theme toggle button using next-themes with sun/moon icons from Lucide, with hydration-safe mounting check.

nodes.name: ai-models.ts
nodes.summary: Multi-model AI abstraction layer providing unified callModel interface for Claude Sonnet 4, GPT-4o, and Grok-3 with token counting and cost calculation in cents.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses OpenAI SDK for both GPT-4o and Grok-3 (via x.ai baseURL), and Anthropic SDK for Claude.

nodes.name: callClaude
nodes.summary: Calls Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4 API with a prompt and optional system message, returning structured ModelResponse with token usage and cost.

nodes.name: callGPT4o
nodes.summary: Calls OpenAI GPT-4o API with a prompt and optional system message, returning structured ModelResponse with token usage and cost.

nodes.name: callGrok
nodes.summary: Calls xAI Grok-3 API via OpenAI-compatible SDK with custom baseURL, returning structured ModelResponse with token usage and cost.

nodes.name: callModel
nodes.summary: Unified dispatcher routing model calls to Claude, GPT-4o, or Grok based on ModelId, with error handling returning structured error responses.

nodes.name: prompts.ts
nodes.summary: Dynasty Companion AI system prompts defining the core identity, FATE advisor context, four archetypes (Steward, Shepherd, Sheepdog, Maker), thirteen historical/biblical advisors, council mode, and RAG context builder.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure data/prompt definition file with no side effects -- all exports are string constants and builder functions.

nodes.name: getArchetypePrompt
nodes.summary: Builds a system prompt string for a specific archetype persona (Steward, Shepherd, Sheepdog, or Maker) with voice, focus, and tone.

nodes.name: getAdvisorPrompt
nodes.summary: Builds a first-person system prompt for a historical or biblical advisor persona with domain expertise and voice characteristics.

nodes.name: getCouncilPrompt
nodes.summary: Builds a dialogue-format system prompt for Council mode pairing two advisors, creating genuine tension and synthesis between perspectives.

nodes.name: buildDDCompanionPrompt
nodes.summary: Master prompt builder composing system identity, archetype/advisor persona, FATE context, user profile, and RAG book passages into a complete companion system prompt.

nodes.name: rag.ts
nodes.summary: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) module for searching the Discreet Dynasties book content using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small embeddings and pgvector similarity search.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma raw SQL with pgvector cosine distance operator (<=>). Lazy-initializes OpenAI client.

nodes.name: searchDDBook
nodes.summary: Performs vector similarity search against DDBookContent table, returning top-K matching passages with chapter numbers for RAG context injection.

nodes.name: auth-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Authorization helper functions for checking DD tier access (canAccessDD, canAccessDDPremium, isDDDynast), tier label mapping, and badge color assignment.

nodes.name: canAccessDD
nodes.summary: Checks whether a user role grants base-level Discreet Dynasties access (Builder, Steward, Dynast, Forge Bundle, Full Arsenal, or Admin).

nodes.name: getTierLabel
nodes.summary: Maps internal role codes to human-readable tier labels (e.g., dd_basic to 'Builder', forge_bundle to 'Forge Bundle') for display in the UI.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Site-wide configuration constants exporting the book URL, site URL, Stoic Preparedness URL, and FT3 author site URL.

nodes.name: content-pipeline.ts
nodes.summary: Automated content generation pipeline that rotates through book chapters and AI models (Claude/GPT-4o/Grok) to produce Hall posts and email dispatches, with cursor-based state tracking, Resend email delivery, and owner notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a cursor-based rotation pattern to alternate content types, cycle through book chapters, and rotate AI models across runs.

nodes.name: generateAndPublish
nodes.summary: Core content generation function that selects next chapter and model from rotation, generates Hall post or email dispatch via AI, publishes to database and sends emails, then advances the cursor.

nodes.name: notifyOwner
nodes.summary: Sends an email notification to the site owner after content auto-publication with a preview and status of what was published (Hall post, email dispatch, etc.).

nodes.name: cron-auth.ts
nodes.summary: Cron route authentication using timing-safe comparison to verify Bearer tokens against both CRON_SECRET (Vercel) and INTERNAL_SECRET (Orchestrator).

nodes.name: verifyCronAuth
nodes.summary: Validates cron/orchestrator authorization by checking the Bearer token against CRON_SECRET and INTERNAL_SECRET using timing-safe string comparison.

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: Singleton Prisma client initialization using the Neon serverless adapter, with global caching for hot-reload safety in development.

nodes.name: book.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the complete 28-chapter structure of the Discreet Dynasties 'Living Book' across five sections (Foundation, FATE, Preps, Long Table, Vow), with tier-gated access and lookup helpers.

nodes.name: fate.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the FATE audit framework (Food, Assurance, Tools, Energy) with 30 scored questions across three levels (Stability, Continuity, Integrity) and scoring/analysis functions.

nodes.name: scoreFateAudit
nodes.summary: Computes per-domain FATE scores by evaluating whether all questions at each level pass, cascading from level 1 through level 3.

nodes.name: pathways.ts
nodes.summary: Defines six guided multi-week pathways (FATE Foundation, Living Preps, Gray Man, Energy Independence, Dynasty Wealth, Quiet Mutual Aid) with weekly actions and reflections for subscriber engagement.

nodes.name: evergreen.ts
nodes.summary: Evergreen social content engine that cycles through published dispatches, extracts quotable passages via AI model rotation, generates platform-native social copy, and manages deduplication and cadence control.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a cursor-based state machine to iterate through dispatch content in chunks, with SHA-256 deduplication and a launch/cruise cadence mode.

nodes.name: getOrCreateCursor
nodes.summary: Retrieves or initializes the evergreen cursor, seeding it with the oldest published dispatch on first run.

nodes.name: getNextChunk
nodes.summary: Advances through published dispatches chunk by chunk, cycling to new posts or new cycles when content is exhausted.

nodes.name: extractPassage
nodes.summary: Uses an AI model to extract the most quotable standalone passage from a dispatch chunk for social media reuse.

nodes.name: generateSocialCopy
nodes.summary: Generates platform-native social media posts (Facebook, X, Instagram) from an extracted passage using AI, returning structured JSON.

nodes.name: shouldRunNow
nodes.summary: Determines whether the evergreen engine should execute based on launch mode (first 14 days, both slots) vs cruise mode (morning slot only).

nodes.name: json-ld.tsx
nodes.summary: JSON-LD structured data generators for SEO across the TFOS family, producing WebSite, Person, BlogPosting, BreadcrumbList, Book, Course, FAQ, and HowTo schema.org markup.

nodes.name: articleJsonLd
nodes.summary: Generates BlogPosting schema.org JSON-LD for dispatch articles with author, dates, word count, and keywords.

nodes.name: courseJsonLd
nodes.summary: Generates Course schema.org JSON-LD for guided pathways with week-based duration.

nodes.name: howToJsonLd
nodes.summary: Generates HowTo schema.org JSON-LD for audit tools and generators with step-by-step structure.

nodes.name: facebook.ts
nodes.summary: Facebook Graph API integration for posting to a Page feed with URL stripping (throttle prevention), collaborator tagging with retry fallback, and first-comment support.

nodes.name: postToFacebook
nodes.summary: Posts content to a Facebook Page feed via Graph API, stripping URLs to avoid throttling and retrying without collaborator tags on permission errors.

nodes.name: commentOnFacebook
nodes.summary: Posts a comment reply to an existing Facebook post via Graph API for first-comment link delivery.

nodes.name: image-picker.ts
nodes.summary: Selects the most appropriate image from the ImagePool database for social media posts using keyword-based theme matching with least-recently-used rotation.

nodes.name: pickImage
nodes.summary: Picks an image from the pool by matching content themes (dynasty, fate keywords), falling back to least-recently-used if no theme match.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Social dispatch orchestrator that fans out content to Facebook, Instagram, and X/Twitter with per-platform error handling, and generates AI-powered first-comment replies with article links.

nodes.name: dispatchToAll
nodes.summary: Dispatches content to all enabled social platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X), collecting per-platform success/failure/skipped results.

nodes.name: postFirstComments
nodes.summary: Generates an AI first-comment via Claude and posts it as a reply on Facebook and X to each successfully dispatched post, delivering article links without throttle risk.

nodes.name: instagram.ts
nodes.summary: Instagram Graph API integration using the two-step container creation and publish flow for image feed posts.

nodes.name: postToInstagram
nodes.summary: Posts an image with caption to Instagram via the Graph API two-step media container creation and publish flow.

nodes.name: metrics.ts
nodes.summary: Fetches post-level engagement metrics (impressions, clicks, likes, retweets) from Facebook Insights and X/Twitter public metrics APIs.

nodes.name: fetchFacebookMetrics
nodes.summary: Retrieves post impressions, engagements, and clicks from the Facebook Graph API Insights endpoint.

nodes.name: fetchXMetrics
nodes.summary: Retrieves tweet-level public metrics (impressions, likes, retweets, replies) via the Twitter API v2.

nodes.name: x.ts
nodes.summary: X/Twitter API v2 integration for posting tweets and threaded replies using OAuth 1.0a credentials.

nodes.name: postToX
nodes.summary: Posts a tweet to X/Twitter via the v2 API using OAuth 1.0a app credentials.

nodes.name: replyOnX
nodes.summary: Posts a threaded reply to an existing tweet on X/Twitter for first-comment link delivery.

nodes.name: story-arc.ts
nodes.summary: Story arc detection and social copy generation for multi-part dispatch series, with optional Grok wit-refinement for X/Twitter posts.

nodes.name: generateArcSocialCopy
nodes.summary: Generates platform-native social copy for multi-part content arcs using Claude, with optional Grok refinement for wittier X posts.

nodes.name: stripe.ts
nodes.summary: Stripe payment integration providing lazy-initialized client, price-to-role mapping for four subscription tiers (Builder, Steward, Dynast, Forge Bundle), and customer creation with DB sync.

nodes.name: getOrCreateStripeCustomer
nodes.summary: Finds or creates a Stripe customer for a user email, syncing the customer ID back to the Prisma User record.

nodes.name: middleware.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js middleware enforcing auth-gated routes, admin-only access, and paid-tier requirements for premium content areas (Armory, Pathways, Long Table).

nodes.name: next-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Next.js TypeScript environment declarations providing global type references.

nodes.name: next.config.js
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration enabling standalone output mode and allowing remote images from Vercel Blob Storage.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Project manifest defining 27 runtime dependencies (Next.js 14, Prisma 7, Stripe, Anthropic SDK, Twitter API, React-PDF, Vercel Blob) with Prisma generate as a build prerequisite.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins for CSS processing.

nodes.name: prisma.config.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma configuration file specifying schema path, migration directory, and Neon serverless datasource URL from environment.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Prisma schema defining 40+ models spanning Auth.js user management, book content with vector embeddings, Stoic companion conversations, practice OS progress tracking, FATE audits, armory generators, dispatch publishing, social media posting, community Hall features, and Stripe subscription billing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pgvector extension for embedding-based RAG retrieval on book content, and the Neon serverless adapter for connection pooling.

nodes.name: seed-book.ts
nodes.summary: Standalone seed script that reads the Discreet Dynasties book markdown, splits it into overlapping 800-word chunks per chapter, generates text-embedding-3-small vectors via OpenAI, and inserts into DDBookContent for RAG retrieval.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates book seeding: locates the markdown file, splits by chapter headings, chunks text with overlap, generates embeddings, and inserts with raw SQL for pgvector compatibility.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration defining the dynasty design system with walnut/amber/parchment color tokens, Crimson Pro display and reading typography, and responsive spacing scale.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration with strict mode, bundler module resolution, path alias (@/*), and Next.js plugin integration.

nodes.name: next-auth.d.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth.js module augmentation extending the Session interface with role, isPaid, username, and avatar fields for subscription-aware authorization.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration defining four cron jobs: publish (every minute), social dispatch (6pm UTC), evergreen (10pm UTC), and content-gen (6am UTC).

layers.name: Pages & UI
layers.description: Next.js page routes, client components, and interactive forms powering the Dynasty Forge user experience -- book reader, FATE audit, Armory generators, Hall community, pathways, and membership flows.

layers.name: API Routes
layers.description: Next.js API route handlers for account management, admin operations, Armory AI generation, billing/checkout, companion chat, community Hall interactions, pathway progression, gift redemption, and Stripe webhook processing.

layers.name: Cron & Content Automation
layers.description: Scheduled cron route handlers and their service modules for automated content generation, dispatch publishing, social media posting, evergreen content recycling, and story arc detection.

layers.name: AI & Companion Services
layers.description: Multi-model AI abstraction (Claude, GPT-4o, Grok), Dynasty Companion advisor prompts and personas, and RAG-powered book search using pgvector embeddings.

layers.name: Social Media Integration
layers.description: Platform-specific API clients for Facebook, Instagram, and X/Twitter posting, engagement metrics collection, image selection from the ImagePool, and cross-platform dispatch orchestration.

layers.name: Domain Models & Data
layers.description: Core domain definitions for the FATE audit framework, 28-chapter book structure, six guided pathways, Prisma database schema with 40+ models, and the Neon serverless database client.

layers.name: Auth & Payments
layers.description: NextAuth v5 configuration with magic link and GitHub providers, tier-based authorization helpers, Stripe subscription management with four-tier pricing, cron route authentication, and request middleware.

layers.name: Configuration & Build
layers.description: Project-level configuration including Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS design system, PostCSS, package manifest, environment variable template, SEO utilities, and site constants.

layers.name: CI/CD & Deployment
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows for governance checks (cron enforcement) and deploy notifications, plus Vercel deployment configuration with four scheduled cron jobs.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project governance (CLAUDE.md), architecture plan (PLAN.md), and onboarding README documenting the site's mission, features, data flows, and deployment process.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Discreet Dynasties is: a TFOS satellite site dedicated to family legacy and stewardship, built around the FATE model (Food, Assurance, Tools, Energy). The README maps out the core features -- the Living Book, FATE Audit, Dynasty Companion AI, Armory generators, guided Pathways, and subscription tiers -- giving you a roadmap for everything you will encounter in this tour.

tour.title: Root Layout and App Shell
tour.description: The root layout bootstraps the entire Next.js application, configuring three custom fonts (Crimson Pro, Outfit, IBM Plex Mono), injecting JSON-LD structured data for SEO, and wrapping the page tree in session and theme providers. It also mounts the Navigation bar and Footer that appear on every page, establishing the Dynasty Forge visual identity.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router layouts are server components by default. The Providers wrapper is a client component boundary that enables SessionProvider and ThemeProvider to work with React context, which only runs on the client.

tour.title: Authentication and Authorization
tour.description: NextAuth v5 powers all authentication, configured with Resend magic-link email and GitHub OAuth providers via the Prisma adapter. The session callback enriches user data with role, paid status, and gift access expiry. The auth-helpers module provides tier-checking functions (canAccessDD, canAccessDDPremium, isDDDynast) used throughout the codebase to gate content by subscription level.

tour.title: Database and Data Schema
tour.description: The Prisma schema defines 40+ models that back the entire application -- from Auth.js user management and book content with pgvector embeddings, to FATE audits, community Hall posts, dispatch publishing, social media tracking, and Stripe billing. The singleton Prisma client uses the Neon serverless adapter, with global caching for hot-reload safety in development. This schema is the single source of truth for every feature in the tour.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma uses a declarative schema language where models map to database tables and relations are expressed through @relation directives. The Neon serverless adapter replaces standard TCP connections with HTTP-based queries optimized for serverless environments like Vercel.

tour.title: The Living Book and RAG System
tour.description: The Discreet Dynasties book (a 4961-line markdown manuscript) is the doctrinal source powering the AI features. The book module defines all 28 chapters across five sections with tier-gated access. The seed script chunks each chapter into overlapping 800-word segments, generates OpenAI text-embedding-3-small vectors, and stores them in the database for pgvector similarity search. The RAG module then retrieves relevant passages when users interact with the Dynasty Companion.
tour.languageLesson: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) grounds AI responses in source material. Overlapping chunks ensure context is not lost at chunk boundaries. pgvector enables cosine similarity search directly in PostgreSQL, avoiding the need for a separate vector database.

tour.title: FATE Audit Framework
tour.description: The FATE audit is the site's cornerstone assessment, scoring users across four domains (Food, Assurance, Tools, Energy) at three levels (Stability, Continuity, Integrity). The fate module defines 30 scored questions and implements the scoring algorithm. The assessment page renders the interactive questionnaire, while the API route persists results to the database. Users see their scores on the Forge dashboard, which drives recommendations throughout the platform.

tour.title: Dynasty Companion AI
tour.description: The Companion is an AI chat interface supporting three conversation modes: archetype-based (Steward, Shepherd, Sheepdog, Maker), single historical advisor (13 personas including Marcus Aurelius and Benjamin Franklin), and council mode where two advisors debate. The prompts module defines each persona's voice and context. The API route handles tier-gated advisor access, RAG-powered book search for grounding, and Anthropic streaming responses.

tour.title: Armory AI Generators
tour.description: The Armory provides 10 specialized AI generator tools (FATE roadmap, stability plan, food storage calculator, dead preps audit, and more) that turn user inputs into actionable plans. The server page defines each generator's configuration with tier-gated access. The client-side ArmoryGenerator component collects inputs via dynamic forms, streams the AI response from Anthropic Claude, and displays results with copy and regenerate actions.

tour.title: Multi-Model AI Abstraction
tour.description: Building on the AI features in Steps 7-8, the multi-model abstraction layer provides a unified callModel interface that dispatches to Claude Sonnet 4, GPT-4o, or Grok-3 with token counting and cost calculation. The content pipeline and evergreen engine rotate through models to produce varied content. This design prevents vendor lock-in and lets different AI personalities emerge in generated content.

tour.title: Pathways and Long Table
tour.description: Pathways are six guided multi-week programs (FATE Foundation, Living Preps, Gray Man, Energy Independence, Dynasty Wealth, Quiet Mutual Aid) with weekly actions and reflections. The Long Table framework presents six concentric levels of household-to-civic resilience with visual progress tracking. Both features use tier-gated access and week-by-week advancement APIs to drive sustained subscriber engagement.

tour.title: Community Hall and Subscriptions
tour.description: The Hall is the paid-members community forum supporting posts, threaded comments, likes, private journal entries, and category tagging. The subscription system redirects to Stoic Preparedness for unified TFOS billing, while Stripe webhooks handle tier upgrades on checkout completion and downgrades on cancellation. The Vow ceremony page adds a commitment ritual for Dynast-tier members with a public registry.

tour.title: Social Media Dispatch Engine
tour.description: The social dispatch system is the highest fan-out subsystem in the codebase. The orchestrator fans content out to Facebook, Instagram, and X/Twitter with per-platform error handling and AI-generated first-comment replies. Each platform module handles its API specifics: Facebook uses Graph API with URL stripping and collaborator tagging, Instagram uses the two-step container publish flow, and X uses OAuth 1.0a for tweet threading.

tour.title: Evergreen Content Engine
tour.description: The evergreen engine is the most connected module in the codebase, tying together the book, AI models, and social dispatch. It cycles through published dispatches, extracts quotable passages via AI with model rotation, generates platform-native social copy, and manages deduplication through passage hashing and cadence control. The story-arc module detects multi-part series and generates cohesive social threads.

tour.title: Cron Jobs and CI/CD
tour.description: Four Vercel cron jobs drive the automated content lifecycle: publish (every minute for scheduled dispatches), social dispatch (6pm UTC), evergreen (10pm UTC), and content-gen (6am UTC). All cron routes use timing-safe token verification accepting both Vercel's CRON_SECRET and the orchestrator's INTERNAL_SECRET. Two GitHub Actions workflows enforce governance -- one blocks local cron entries in vercel.json, the other sends deploy notifications.
tour.languageLesson: Vercel cron jobs are defined declaratively in vercel.json and invoke API route handlers via GET requests. Timing-safe comparison (crypto.timingSafeEqual) prevents timing attacks when verifying authentication tokens -- standard string equality leaks information about which characters matched.
